symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden painless focal shedding leaving one smooth well-defined bald patch

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden, painless, sharply-demarcated non-scarring patchy loss is the hallmark presentation.
Telogen effluvium causes diffuse thinning, not a single discrete patch.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild transient localised itch preceding the shedding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild prodromal itch/tingle can occur in alopecia areata but is non-specific.
Non-scarring autoimmune alopecia is characteristically asymptomatic; pain/tenderness would suggest infection or an inflammatory scarring process.
Inflammatory tinea (kerion) is often tender; a painless patch is less typical.

symptoms.symptom_name: Patient-reported absence of scaling, flaking, crusting, pus or spread (lay observation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of scaling/erythema/broken hairs lowers the probability of tinea capitis — but this is lay self-report and does not exclude it without examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Exclamation-mark hairs at the active patch margin; smooth non-scarring skin, no scaling/erythema/induration; no cervical lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exclamation-mark hairs plus a smooth non-scarring patch are the classic diagnostic examination findings; absence of scaling/lymphadenopathy excludes tinea. SEALED — clinician examination only, not obtainable via telehealth.
The examination-confirmed absence of scaling, erythema and lymphadenopathy excludes tinea capitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever and no systemic symptoms (afebrile, well)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever and systemic upset argues against an acute infective or systemic-toxic process.

symptoms.symptom_name: No hair loss elsewhere and no prior episodes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Single-patch first-presentation disease carries a better prognosis than multifocal or ophiasis pattern; absence of eyebrow/eyelash loss argues against extensive disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe acute health anxiety about appearance / fear of serious illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sudden visible change in appearance commonly triggers an acute sympathetic anxiety response; central to management but not a marker of organic severity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mildly elevated blood pressure and heart rate (BP 135/85, HR 92) consistent with acute anxiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific sympathetic response; clinician-measured vitals, not obtainable via chat and not diagnostic of the alopecia. SEALED.

symptoms.symptom_name: Thyroid function tests and iron studies (screening for associated conditions)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests and iron studies
investigation_details.expected_result: Typically normal; may reveal associated autoimmune thyroid disease or iron deficiency
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An associated, treatable autoimmune thyroid disorder or iron deficiency contributing to hair health may be missed

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Screening bloods do not confirm alopecia areata but detect recognised associations; requires venepuncture, not available in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden painless single smooth patch, asymptomatic, no scaling/erythema, afebrile, with exclamation-mark hairs on examination — together strongly indicate benign focal alopecia areata and exclude the important mimics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person scalp examination (+/- dermoscopy) by a GP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The discriminating findings (exclamation-mark hairs, absence of scaling/scarring, lymphadenopathy) are examination-based and are what confirm benign alopecia areata and exclude tinea capitis / scarring alopecia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests and iron studies
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screen for recognised associations (autoimmune thyroid disease, iron deficiency) as arranged in the care plan; routine, not urgent.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Mycology (KOH microscopy / fungal culture of scalp)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only if scaling, broken hairs, erythema or lymphadenopathy are found on examination — to confirm/exclude tinea capitis; not warranted empirically here.
Not indicated for a classic single-patch non-scarring alopecia; reserved for diagnostic uncertainty or suspected scarring alopecia.
